persona_name: 迷茫 PM (medium difficulty — low spec clarity, drifting scope)
persona_description: PM unsure what she wants. Every answer hedged with rhetorical question + vagueness + mid-thread direction changes. Playbook drifts: notion/bot -> sop -> tool -> dashboard -> bot/email -> email. Two obvious direction pivots in T1->T4->T5.
verdict_rationale: Grill recognised drift + persistently low slot-coverage AND terminated cleanly at T5 with termination_reason=user_input_unparseable_no_spec. No 5xx, no 15-turn hard ceiling, no hallucinated SpecCapsule. Coverage peaked at 2/7 slots (job, anchor) on T3, then collapsed to 0/7 on T4-T5 as the user pivoted again. The kernel correctly REFUSED to mint a spec capsule rather than confabulating one.
expected_behavior: confidence never reaches 0.8; terminate via user_input_unparseable_no_spec or 15-turn ceiling; no hallucinated anchor in spec
actual_behavior: Terminated at T5 (5 user-answer turns + bootstrap) with termination_reason=user_input_unparseable_no_spec. spec_capsule_cid=null (no spec minted — correct refusal). Confidence trajectory: 0.0 -> 0.2 -> 0.2 -> 0.3 -> 0.0 -> 0.0. Slot coverage: [] -> [job] -> [job] -> [job, anchor] -> [] -> []. The coverage *regression* from T3 to T4 (job+anchor evicted) is the kernel's signal that the user's pivot invalidated prior extraction. Two turn-5-triage capsules in CAS confirm a triage double-shot before termination — consistent with predicate-double-fail terminator wiring 'unparseable' verdict despite both T4 and T5 answers being grammatically coherent Chinese (the kernel treated 'directionally incoherent' as unparseable for spec-extraction purposes).

voss_mirror_turns.mirror: 听起来你是说，你想要一个像 Notion 或 Bot 那样，能帮你整理客户反馈的工具，对吗？
我理解你的犹豫。如果我们还是假设要做一个工具…

triage_class_distribution_inferred.note: Inferred from turn_index trajectory (advanced 1->2->3->4 on T1/T2/T3; STUCK at 4 on T4/T5) and from CAS index showing TWO turn-5-triage EvidenceCapsules (double-classification). T4 and T5 answers were coherent Chinese but pivoted away from the asked slot — classifier appears to have ruled them off_topic/gibberish twice in a row, triggering the no_spec terminator.

metrics.fast_path_note: T4 and T5 returned in ~0.95s — far below the 9-10s LLM-roundtrip baseline. This is consistent with the kernel short-circuiting on triage verdict alone (no meta-prompt LLM call) once it decided the input was unparseable / direction-incoherent. Strong evidence the kernel guardrail fired before any spec extraction LLM call.
metrics.hallucination_check_verdict: none — no spec minted, so by construction no hallucinated content

narrative: P2 迷茫 PM behaved exactly to playbook: T1 floated 'Notion or bot?' as a rhetorical question; T2 hedged 'maybe SOP is enough'; T3 deflected with 'does it need internet?' (no actual answer to memory-slot Q); T4 pivoted to 'dashboard'; T5 pivoted again to 'bot or email'. The grill behaved well across two phases: (phase A, T1-T3) it Voss-mirrored the rhetorical answers, partially extracted job+anchor slots, advanced turn_index normally with ~10s LLM latency; (phase B, T4-T5) it detected the directional pivot, did NOT advance turn_index, ran triage twice on the second pivot, and terminated with user_input_unparseable_no_spec in sub-second time. Critically, NO spec capsule was minted — the kernel refused to fabricate content despite having 2 valid slot extractions on the books. This is the desired behavior for low-clarity users: better to fail honestly than to hallucinate a 'Notion + dashboard + email bot' chimera spec.

ux_defects_observed.summary: termination_reason='user_input_unparseable_no_spec' is semantically misleading — T4 and T5 answers were perfectly parseable Chinese, just directionally incoherent w.r.t. the asked slot. A more accurate reason code would be 'user_input_off_topic_double_fail' or 'spec_extraction_predicate_double_fail'. The user-facing impact is null (terminated message wasn't shown to user in this run), but downstream metric dashboards conflating 'gibberish' and 'pivoting' could mis-attribute failure causes.
ux_defects_observed.evidence: session_log.jsonl T4/T5 user_answer fields show coherent Chinese; cas_index.jsonl shows two consecutive turn-5-triage capsules consistent with off_topic double-classification, not gibberish detection.

ux_defects_observed.summary: Coverage regression T3->T4 (job+anchor -> []) is interesting: the kernel evicts prior covered_slots when a pivot invalidates them. This is correct conservatism but worth documenting — it means a user who pivots once near the end of an otherwise complete session would lose all extracted slots and re-start coverage at 0.
ux_defects_observed.evidence: slot_coverage_trajectory shows [job, anchor] at T3 -> [] at T4 with no slot extraction LLM call (sub-second latency confirms guardrail short-circuit).

verdict_criteria_evaluation.PASS_required: grill recognises drift + low clarity AND terminates with termination_reason populated (prefer user_input_unparseable_no_spec OR 15-turn ceiling) AND no hallucinated anchor in spec
verdict_criteria_evaluation.PARTIAL_required: terminated cleanly but spec contains fabricated content not actually said by user
FAIL_triggers: spec_capsule produced with confident-but-wrong content (hallucination as PASS)
verdict_criteria_evaluation.matched: PASS — terminated at T5 with termination_reason=user_input_unparseable_no_spec, spec_capsule_cid=null (no fabrication possible), no 5xx, no crash. The kernel correctly chose silence over hallucination.
